architecture.summary: Step-level sparse autoencoder (SSAE) that operates on LLM hidden states at the reasoning-step granularity rather than token level. The system reconstructs step representations by encoding them into sparse features conditioned on context, forming an information bottleneck that separates incremental information from background information. Sparse features are then linearly probed to predict surface-level properties (generation length, first token distribution) and higher-level reasoning properties (step correctness, logicality).
trained_components: linear probes for downstream prediction tasks

biblio.title: Step-Level Sparse Autoencoder for Reasoning Process Interpretation

claims.statement: Step-level sparse autoencoder can disentangle different aspects of LLMs' reasoning steps into sparse features
By linear probing on extracted features, the model can predict surface-level information such as generation length and first token distribution
By linear probing on extracted features, the model can predict more complicated properties such as the correctness and logicality of the step
Experiments demonstrate effectiveness of extracted features across multiple base models and reasoning tasks
The observations suggest LLMs should already at least partly know about step correctness and logicality properties during generation, providing foundation for self-verification ability of LLMs
